title: Populate-GridByKey assigns PSObject-wrapped values to typed DataTable columns without explicit cast — BooleanStorage.Set InvalidCastException
description: In Invoke-PSEnvironmentScanner.ps1 Populate-GridByKey (~L2134-2165), DataTable columns are correctly typed from PSObject property TypeNameOfValue (e.g. [bool] for System.Boolean). However, when populating rows via $row = $dt.NewRow() / $row[$p.Name] = $val, the value $val is retrieved as a raw PSObject-wrapped property via $item.($p.Name). No explicit cast is applied before the row-column assignment. The DataTable's BooleanStorage.Set (and IntStorage.Set etc.) receive a PSObject instead of the underlying .NET primitive, throwing System.InvalidCastException. This differs from P026's documented path (.Rows.Add() with positional args) — here the $row[$p.Name] = $val pattern is the vector. The column type-detection code is correct; only the value-assignment step is missing the unwrap cast.
reported_by: User (runtime exception from scan tool UI)
resolution: Fixed in Invoke-PSEnvironmentScanner.ps1 Populate-GridByKey: inside the foreach ($p in $props) loop, added $colType = $dt.Columns[$p.Name].DataType and explicit branch casts ([bool], [int], [long], [double], else [string]) before $row[$p.Name] = $val. PSObject is now unwrapped by PowerShell's cast operator before DataTable's BooleanStorage.Set receives the value.
detection_method: User-reported unhandled WinForms exception during scan-tool button click. Stack trace explicitly names Populate-GridByKey. Confirmed by reading code — no [bool]$val or [int]$val cast applied before $row[$p.Name] = $val assignment inside the foreach ($p in $props) loop.
root_cause: PSObject wrapping survives the $item.($p.Name) property access. PowerShell does not auto-unwrap PSObject when assigning to a typed System.Data.DataColumn via $row[col] = val. The DataTable's column-specific Storage.Set method calls System.Convert.ChangeType internally (in .NET) but PSObject does not implement IConvertible, causing the cast chain to fail. Explicit [bool]$val / [int]$val in PowerShell correctly calls PSObject.BaseObject before the .NET layer receives the value.
exception_message: System.InvalidCastException: Unable to cast object of type 'System.Management.Automation.PSObject' to type 'System.IConvertible'. Couldn't store <False> in Enabled Column. Expected type is Boolean.
affected_columns_observed: Any column whose TypeNameOfValue resolves to System.Boolean, System.Int32, System.Int64, System.Double in scan result PSObjects
